filename: # Fill in your image filenames and corresponding pixel sizes (micrometers per pixel).
# If you leave this file empty or don't provide it
# diameter etc.) will be reported in pixels instead of micrometers.
# How to find your pixel size:
#   - If your images have a scale bar: measure the scale bar in pixels in ImageJ
#     (Analyze -> Measure)
um_per_px: then divide the known length by the pixel count.

filename: #     Example: scale bar = 298 pixels = 50 um -> 50/298 = 0.168 um/px
#   - If you know your objective magnification: check your microscope camera's
#     specification sheet for sensor pixel size
#   - In Zeiss ZEN: shown in the bottom status bar as "X.XX um/px"
#   - In Leica LAS: shown in Image Properties
# Example entries (delete these and add your own):
# image001.tif
# image002.tif
# MA_sample1.tif
# Notes:
#   - Filenames must match exactly (including extension)
#   - Images not listed here will have metrics reported in pixels
#   - You can use the same pixel size for all images from the same
#     microscope/objective combination
